resultRows.description: The public sample block discusses avoiding trans fats, limiting saturated fats, and replacing them with healthier unsaturated fats for triglyceride context.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Triglycerides. Use this as Cardiovascular Health output-format evidence only; it is not a local diagnosis, cardiovascular risk estimate, diet recommendation, supplement recommendation, exercise plan, medication recommendation, lab interpretation, imaging recommendation, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.description: The public sample block says consuming unsaturated fats is associated with lower ApoB levels.

practicalNextSteps: Review cardiovascular questions with a clinician who can use labs, blood pressure, family history, medications, and full clinical context.
Use the appendix to see which cardiovascular model, clinical, and recommendation inputs were unavailable.
Do not use sample recommendation rows or derived genotype evidence alone to change diet, supplements, exercise, medication, testing, or treatment.

references.title: NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores

genotypeSummary.effect: Sample variant row for LDL particle size recommendation context
genotypeSummary.phenotype: The sample prose says healthy fats may increase LDL particle size more in people with the sample APOE variant.

genotypeSummary.effect: Sample variant row for fiber and cholesterol recommendation context
genotypeSummary.phenotype: The sample prose says beta-glucans from oats or barley may reduce cholesterol more in people with the sample CYP7A1 variant.

genotypeSummary.effect: Sample variant row for fiber and triglyceride recommendation context
genotypeSummary.phenotype: The sample prose says psyllium supplementation may reduce triglycerides more in people with the sample FABP2 variant.

probabilities.label: Calibrated personal probability
probabilities.note: The deterministic report body does not quantify disease risk, diagnosis probability, treatment response, recommendation benefit, or actionability probabilities.

uncertainty: Sample-report rows are output-format evidence, not local clinical or lifestyle decisions.
Derived or unavailable-model evidence does not include the clinical, lab, environment, medication, and professional-review context needed for personal decisions.
Missing inputs are listed explicitly instead of being inferred.

limitations: This local artifact uses public sample-report structure and synthetic derived evidence for validation.
It does not diagnose familial hypercholesterolemia, coronary artery disease, heart attack, stroke, high cholesterol, high lipoprotein(a), or blood-pressure conditions.
It does not include lipid labs, blood pressure, diabetes, smoking, medication list, age/sex, family history, calibrated ASCVD model, clinician interpretation, or personal recommendation evidence.
